hero.headline: Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA in Human Resource Management: Fees, Syllabus and Career Scope
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme | NAAC A accredited | Don Bosco/Salesian educational network

sections.tldr: Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA in Human Resource Management covers Applied Psychology in Personnel Administration, Compensation Management, Training and Development, Team Dynamics and Leadership, and Transformational Leadership in Sem 3. Sem 4 includes Organizational Staffing and HR Analytics, Performance Management, Organizational Change and Development, Strategic Human Resource Management, and Cross Cultural Management. NAAC A accredited, UGC-DEB approved. Part of the Don Bosco/Salesian educational network. Startup Management is a common Sem 3 core.

about.body:
Assam Don Bosco University (CDOE) is a private university in Guwahati, Assam, operating under the globally recognized Don Bosco/Salesian educational network. The Salesian order runs more than 1,500 educational institutions across 130+ countries. ADB holds NAAC A accreditation and UGC-DEB approval for its online programmes.

The HRM specialisation is one of the most subject-dense among ADB's MBA offerings. Sem 3 places five dedicated HR subjects: Applied Psychology in Personnel Administration, Compensation Management, Training and Development, Team Dynamics and Leadership, and Transformational Leadership. Sem 4 continues with five more: Organizational Staffing and HR Analytics, Performance Management, Organizational Change and Development, Strategic Human Resource Management, and Cross Cultural Management.

The inclusion of both Team Dynamics and Leadership alongside Transformational Leadership in Sem 3 shows a strong leadership development focus. Cross Cultural Management in Sem 4 is relevant for professionals working with diverse Northeast Indian communities or in multinational contexts.

Startup Management appears as a common Sem 3 core subject. This is uncommon at the NAAC A tier and useful for HR professionals advising early-stage companies on people strategy.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA core. Semesters 3-4 below show the HRM specialisation subjects along with common Sem 3-4 core subjects.

subjects.name: Applied Psychology in Personnel Administration
subjects.desc: Psychological principles in HR, employee motivation theories, workplace behaviour analysis, and psychometric assessment in hiring.

subjects.name: Compensation Management
subjects.desc: Salary structuring, pay-for-performance systems, benefits administration, executive compensation, and compensation benchmarking.

subjects.name: Training and Development
subjects.desc: Training needs analysis, programme design and delivery, e-learning methods, ROI of training, and employee development plans.

subjects.name: Team Dynamics and Leadership
subjects.desc: Group behaviour, team formation stages, conflict resolution, leadership styles, situational leadership, and high-performance team building.

subjects.name: Transformational Leadership
subjects.desc: Transformational vs transactional leadership, vision-driven management, change leadership, inspirational motivation, and intellectual stimulation.

subjects.name: Minor Project
subjects.desc: Guided research project on an HR-related topic. Problem identification, methodology design, and initial findings.

subjects.name: Startup Management
subjects.desc: Startup ecosystem, business model canvas, funding stages (seed, angel, VC), lean startup methodology, and go-to-market strategy.

subjects.name: Operations Management
subjects.desc: Production planning, quality management, supply chain basics, inventory control, and process optimization.

subjects.name: Organizational Staffing and HR Analytics
subjects.desc: Recruitment strategy, workforce planning, talent acquisition metrics, HR data analysis, and predictive analytics for attrition and engagement.

subjects.name: Performance Management
subjects.desc: Performance appraisal systems, KPI design, 360-degree feedback, balanced scorecard approach, and performance improvement plans.

subjects.name: Organizational Change and Development
subjects.desc: Change management frameworks, OD interventions, organizational culture transformation, resistance management, and planned change processes.

subjects.name: Strategic Human Resource Management
subjects.desc: HR as a strategic partner, workforce strategy alignment with business goals, HR scorecard, and evidence-based HR decision making.

subjects.name: Cross Cultural Management
subjects.desc: Cultural dimensions (Hofstede, Trompenaars), managing diverse teams, cross-cultural communication, expatriate management, and global HR practices.

subjects.name: Business Policy and Strategic Management
subjects.desc: Strategic analysis frameworks, competitive positioning, corporate-level strategy, and strategic implementation and control.

subjects.name: Research Project/Dissertation
subjects.desc: Full research dissertation on an HR topic. Data collection, analysis, findings, and structured thesis presentation.

subjects.name: Computer Applications in Business
subjects.desc: Business software tools, data analysis using spreadsheets, MIS reporting, and technology-driven decision support systems.

whoHires.body:
ADB MBA HRM graduates target roles across talent management, organizational development, and HR operations:

**Industries:** IT/ITES, manufacturing, BFSI, healthcare, hospitality, government and PSU HR departments, NGOs, and development sector organizations

**Entry-level roles:** HR Executive, Recruitment Coordinator, Training Coordinator, Payroll Officer, HR Analyst, Employee Engagement Associate

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years):** HR Manager, Talent Acquisition Manager, L&D Manager, Compensation and Benefits Manager, HR Business Partner, OD Specialist

**Senior roles (7+ years):** CHRO, VP Human Resources, Director of Talent, Head of L&D, Director of Organizational Development, Head of HR Strategy

**Employers:** IT companies (TCS, Infosys, Wipro), manufacturing firms, banking HR teams, NE India development organizations, tea industry corporates, and government/PSU HR departments.

**Note:** NE India's growing service sector and development organizations create steady HR demand. Cross Cultural Management skills are directly relevant to the region's diverse ethnic and linguistic landscape.

skills.body:
The HRM specialisation builds skills across people management, analytics, and organizational development:

**Technical skills:**
- HR Analytics: workforce data analysis, attrition prediction models, recruitment funnel metrics, and HR dashboard creation
- Compensation Design: salary benchmarking, pay band structuring, CTC computation, benefits package design, and executive compensation frameworks
- Performance Systems: KPI framework design, 360-degree feedback implementation, PIP documentation, and balanced scorecard metrics
- Training and Development: TNA methodology, training programme ROI calculation, e-learning platform evaluation, and competency mapping

**Applied skills:**
- Organizational Development: change management project execution, culture assessment surveys, OD intervention design, and restructuring support
- Cross Cultural Management: cultural assessment tools, expatriate onboarding programme design, and diverse team facilitation

**Soft skills:** Conducting difficult conversations (PIPs, exits), facilitating leadership workshops, presenting HR metrics to C-suite, and mediating workplace conflicts across cultural contexts.

comparisons.body:
Comparing ADB Online MBA HRM against peer programmes:

**vs Uttaranchal Online MBA HRM (Rs 94,000, NAAC A+):** Uttaranchal offers 8 specialisations with NAAC A+ at a fixed Rs 94,000. ADB brings 10 dedicated HRM subjects across Sem 3-4 (five per semester), which is one of the highest HR subject counts among online MBAs. ADB also offers Transformational Leadership and Cross Cultural Management as dedicated subjects. Choose Uttaranchal for fee transparency, ADB for depth of HR curriculum.

**vs Sharda Online MBA HRM (Rs 1,54,500, NAAC A+):** Sharda is significantly more expensive with NCR positioning. ADB offers more dedicated HR subjects and Don Bosco heritage at a lower likely fee point. Sharda wins on Greater Noida/NCR corporate access. ADB wins on curriculum depth and global educational network.

**vs Galgotias Online MBA HRM (Rs 86,400, NAAC A+):** Galgotias is budget-tier with NCR location. ADB offers Applied Psychology, Transformational Leadership, and Cross Cultural Management as standalone subjects, which Galgotias typically does not. Galgotias wins on published fee and NCR brand. ADB wins on HR specialisation depth.

**vs Vignan Online MBA HRM (Rs 90,000, NAAC A+):** Vignan offers a flat Rs 90,000 fee with South India focus. ADB offers more dedicated HR subjects and Don Bosco/Salesian network recognition. Both are budget-tier. Choose based on regional preference and HR curriculum depth.

fees.body:
The Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA in Human Resource Management fee structure is confirmed at admission. Please reconfirm current fees, payment options, and any applicable charges with our counsellor before enrolment.

Assam Don Bosco University is a UGC-DEB approved NAAC A accredited institution in Guwahati, part of the globally recognized Don Bosco/Salesian educational network. Financing options including zero-cost EMI may be available. Confirm terms with counsellor.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with ADB University each quarter. Fees listed on our pages are indicative pending verification each admission cycle.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Human Resource Management as the specialisation, issued by Assam Don Bosco University under UGC-DEB online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A accreditation and UGC-DEB approval. The university is part of the Don Bosco/Salesian educational network with global recognition across 130+ countries. Valid for government recruitment, PSU HR roles, corporate HR positions, and professional HR certifications (SHRM, HRCI) eligibility support.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from ADB Online MBA HRM students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Debajit
items.liked: The Don Bosco educational heritage adds credibility that other NE India universities cannot match. Ten dedicated HR subjects across two semesters is strong
items.disliked: Fee is not publicly listed on the website. Required a call to admissions for the exact amount
items.body: I selected ADB specifically for the Don Bosco name. The Salesian network is recognized globally, and that matters when your resume reaches HR teams outside NE India. Applied Psychology in Personnel Administration and Cross Cultural Management were directly useful in my role managing teams across Assam and Meghalaya. Startup Management as a core subject was a bonus.

items.name: Merina
items.liked: Cross Cultural Management as a dedicated subject is directly relevant for anyone working in NE India's multi-ethnic work environments
items.disliked: Some recorded sessions could be more interactive. Faculty response time varies
items.body: Working in Manipur, I needed an MBA that understood the NE India context. ADB delivered on this. Cross Cultural Management and Team Dynamics subjects addressed real challenges I face daily. HR Analytics in Sem 4 added the data skills my role increasingly demands. The dissertation required structured research, which was time-intensive but valuable.

items.name: Kamal
items.liked: Compensation Management and Performance Management covered practical frameworks I could apply immediately at work
items.disliked: Brand recognition outside NE India is limited. The LMS interface needs modernization
items.body: The HR curriculum is thorough. Ten dedicated subjects plus a dissertation is more than most online MBA HRM programmes offer. The content is functional. The trade-off is brand recognition outside the Northeast. In NE India, Don Bosco is a respected name. Nationally, you may need to explain the university's background to hiring managers.

faqs.question: What is Assam Don Bosco University's affiliation?
faqs.answer: Assam Don Bosco University is part of the Don Bosco/Salesian educational network, a globally recognized Catholic educational order operating 1,500+ institutions across 130+ countries. The university is a private institution in Guwahati, Assam.

faqs.question: Is ADB Online MBA UGC-DEB approved?
faqs.answer: Yes. Assam Don Bosco University holds UGC-DEB approval for its online programmes. The MBA degree is legally equivalent to a campus MBA under UGC 2020 guidelines.

faqs.question: Why is the fee structure confirmed at admission?
faqs.answer: ADB's fee structure varies by admission cycle and payment plan. The exact fee, semester breakdown, and any applicable charges are confirmed during the admission process. Contact our counsellor for the current cycle's fee details.

faqs.question: How many dedicated HR subjects does this programme include?
faqs.answer: Ten dedicated HRM subjects across Sem 3 and Sem 4 (five per semester). This is one of the highest HR subject counts among online MBA HRM programmes. Subjects range from Applied Psychology to Cross Cultural Management.

faqs.question: Is Cross Cultural Management relevant for NE India professionals?
faqs.answer: Yes. NE India has diverse ethnic, linguistic, and cultural communities. Cross Cultural Management covers cultural dimensions, diverse team management, and cross-cultural communication. Directly applicable to workplaces in Assam, Meghalaya, Manipur, and other NE states.

chooseIf: Don Bosco/Salesian educational heritage and global network recognition matter to you.
You want 10 dedicated HRM subjects including Applied Psychology, Transformational Leadership, and Cross Cultural Management.
You are based in NE India and want a NAAC A accredited local university with UGC-DEB approval.
Cross Cultural Management as a standalone subject adds value for your multi-ethnic work environment.
consider: Uttaranchal at Rs 94,000 with NAAC A+ offers a clear published fee and 8 specialisations.
Galgotias at Rs 86,400 with NAAC A+ provides NCR brand positioning at a lower published fee.
Vignan at Rs 90,000 with NAAC A+ offers a fixed published fee with South India recognition.
If national brand recognition is your top priority, Amity, NMIMS, or MUJ have stronger pan-India positioning.
